prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a zink has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the zink-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a zink, a ikk, and a twix. The zink is underneath the ikk. The ikk is to-the-left-of the twix. The zink has a red button on top, a yellow button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The ikk has a yellow button on top and a red button on bottom. The twix has a purple button on top and a red button on side. Pressing the red button on the zink activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a prit, a oxim, and a klon. The klon is underneath the prit. The prit is to-the-left-of the oxim. The prit has a red button on top, a red button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The oxim has a purple button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The klon has a red button on top, a yellow button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The prit is famous for its prominent red button.

Which object in the perception scenario is the zink-analog, and which button activates it?
